questions.question: For two discrete random variables X and Y, the joint probability distribution P(X, Y) is given by P(X=1, Y=2) = 0.3 and P(X=2, Y=3) = 0.2. Find P(X=1 or Y=3).
questions.hint: Use the formula for the sum of probabilities

questions.question: Two coins are tossed simultaneously. Let X be the number of heads and Y be the number of tails. Find P(X=1, Y=1) and P(X=0, Y=2) using a table of all possible outcomes.
questions.hint: List all possible outcomes and calculate the probabilities

questions.question:
Let X be a discrete random variable with the following joint probability distribution:

| X | Y | P(X, Y) |
|---|---|---------|
| 1 | 1 | 0.2     |
| 1 | 2 | 0.3     |
| 2 | 1 | 0.1     |
| 2 | 2 | 0.4     |
Find P(X=1 or Y=1) using the formula for the sum of probabilities.
questions.hint: Use the formula for the sum of probabilities
